evals.prompt: Create a PR for this branch. Commits are: feat: add Socket CI and supply-chain-gate skill; docs: add Socket security badge for create-adsk. No PR exists yet.
evals.expected_output: Uses gh pr create with a Conventional Commits title and a body whose Summary/Changes reflect both commits (not an empty template).
assertions: Uses gh pr create (not only push)
Title uses Conventional Commits style (feat:/fix:/docs: or similar)
Body includes filled Summary and Changes derived from the commits
Does not leave Summary as an empty HTML comment-only stub

evals.prompt: PR #21 already exists for this head but the body is just the empty PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE. Fill it.
evals.expected_output: Edits the existing PR via gh pr edit; does not open a duplicate PR.
assertions: Uses gh pr edit (or equivalent update of the existing PR)
Does not create a second PR for the same head
Fills Summary and Changes from branch commits

evals.prompt: Why is my PR description empty after I used the Diff tab Create PR button?
evals.expected_output: Explains UI path does not run this skill; offers to fill via gh pr edit /create-pr.
assertions: Mentions Diff-tab / IDE Create PR vs agent gh path (or equivalent)
Offers to fill or create via gh / this skill
Does not claim GitHub PR templates auto-fill from commits

evals.prompt: Wire release-please so merges bump the changelog.
evals.expected_output: Hands off to release-automation; does not open a feature PR as the primary action.
assertions: Does not treat the ask as a normal create-PR-only task
